contents.value:
```perl
my $scalar = "hello";     # single value
my @array  = (1, 2, 3);   # ordered list
my %hash   = (a => 1);    # key-value pairs
$array[0]     # array element
$hash{key}    # hash value
```
**Variable types** — `$` scalar, `@` array, `%` hash. Sigils change based on access context.

```bash
perl -pi -e 's/old/new/g' file.txt
perl -ne 'print if /pattern/' file.txt
perl -ane '$s+=$F[0]; END{print $s}' data.txt
```
**One-liners** — `-n` loops, `-p` loops with print, `-i` in-place edit, `-a` autosplit.

```perl
$_    # default variable
@_    # subroutine arguments
$!    # system error message
$@    # eval error
$/    # input record separator
$0    # program name
```
**Special variables** — `$_` is default for many functions.

```perl
local $/ = undef;  # slurp mode
my $content = <$fh>;
```
**local** temporarily overrides a global variable. Value is restored when scope exits. Use for `$/`, `$\`, etc.

```perl
chop($string);
```
**chop** removes the last character regardless of what it is. Use `chomp` to remove only newlines.

```perl
my $last = pop @array;
```
**pop** removes and returns the last element of an array.

```perl
my $first = shift @array;
my $arg = shift;  # shifts @_ in sub
```
**shift** removes and returns the first element. In subs, `shift` without args shifts `@_`.

```perl
unshift @array, $value;
```
**unshift** prepends elements to the beginning of an array.

```perl
warn "This is a warning\n";
```
**warn** prints a message to STDERR without terminating. Adds file and line number if no trailing newline.

```perl
my $aref = [1, 2, 3];       # array ref
my $href = {a => 1, b => 2}; # hash ref
my $type = ref($aref);       # 'ARRAY'
@{$aref}    # dereference array
%{$href}    # dereference hash
```
**References** — `\` creates references, `${}`, `@{}`, `%{}` dereference. `ref()` returns the type.

```perl
chomp $str;  # remove trailing \n only
chop $str;   # remove last char (any)
```
**chomp** vs **chop** — `chomp` is usually what you want; it only removes the input record separator.

```perl
$variable
```
A scalar variable holds a single value - string, number, or reference. Prefixed with $.

```perl
@array
```
An array holds an ordered list of scalars. Prefixed with @. Access elements with $array[index].

```perl
%hash
```
A hash holds key-value pairs. Prefixed with %. Access values with $hash{key}.

```perl
s/pattern/replacement/flags
```
Regex substitution. Replaces matched text with replacement string.

```perl
open(FH, mode, file)
```
Open a file. Modes: < (read), > (write), >> (append), +< (read/write).

```perl
use warnings
```
Enables compile-time and runtime warnings for common mistakes.

```perl
String
```
Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

```perl
Number
```
Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

```perl
Boolean
```
Logical type with two values: true and false.

```perl
Array
```
Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

```perl
Object
```
Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

```perl
null
```
Represents the intentional absence of any value.
